prompt: Give me a short pep talk before I start a hard task I have been putting off all morning.
response:
Let's get this done. That task hasn't gone anywhere, and neither have you - and there's no better time than NOW to start. Think of it like climbing a mountain: the view from the top is amazing, but you have to start putting one foot in front of the other.

Don't worry about how big it is, just focus on one small step at a time. That first action is always the hardest, but once you begin, momentum will take over.

Forget about yesterday - today is a new start. You've been putting this off, but procrastination only makes the task loom larger in your mind. The reality is, you can handle this. You've handled hard things before, and you'll handle this one too.

Stop talking yourself out of it and start doing. Five minutes of focused work will feel better than five hours of avoidance. The feeling of completion will far outweigh the temporary discomfort of starting.

GO. Now. Tackle that task like the capable person you are. You've got this.
